description: A snug single-room tavern: a long bar counter runs the length of the north wall and two small single-tile tables hug the side walls. A two-tile entrance opens onto the south. The room is small enough that every word at the bar carries.
customIntroduction: A runner from the licensing office pressed a docket into your hand at dawn: a tenement yard in the Loom Quarter has been throwing off readings — small ones, the kind a frightened person makes without meaning to. Someone is casting without a licence. Your orders are standard: Reveal, then Regulate. Bring them in breathing if you can.
customContext:
The encounter takes place in and around a cramped tenement block on the edge of the Loom Quarter — a neighbourhood of narrow corridors, locked interior rooms, and a rear yard that opens onto a rubbish-strewn alley. The outer shell of the building is a ring of close walls; the interior is a single gutted common room whose windows have been boarded up from inside. The yard behind is split: a covered passage runs beneath the eaves on the left, and the wide alley at the bottom is the only clean exit. Laundry lines, broken crates, and the smell of tallow and old smoke.

The target is a young elf — a weaver's apprentice — who has been involuntarily manifesting minor light-magic in her sleep, her inherent ability surfacing under stress. She has no idea how to stop. Two of her neighbours, a pair of off-duty imperial soldiers who moonlight as neighbourhood enforcers, have already found her and are holding her in the interior common room, intending to hand her to the Concordat themselves for the bounty rather than wait for a Cape. They are not hostile on sight — they think they have done the law a favour — but they are armed, jumpy, and will turn ugly if they feel their prize being taken without payment. A third soldier patrols the outer corridor, keeping the building locked down.

A fourth figure lurks in the alley: a Concordat overseer who arrived independently, tipped off by a neighbour's note, and who has his own paperwork and his own claim on the girl. He will not attack the player, but he will assert jurisdiction loudly and may attempt to simply walk out with her if the player is distracted.

The girl herself — marked in the encounter as a neutral villager — is terrified and non-violent. If she panics, she may involuntarily cast again.

**Tactical shape:** The outer corridor is a chokepoint; the interior common room is the objective; the alley is the escape route or the fallback. The two soldiers inside are the primary obstacle. The patrolling soldier in the corridor is an early warning system and a potential flanker. The Concordat overseer is a social and procedural threat, not a combat one — but leaving him unsatisfied has downstream consequences.

**The real question** the encounter poses is not whether the player can overpower two guards — they probably can — but what they do with the girl when they have her. File the Quota paperwork, hand her to the Concordat, let her run, or find a third option the law does not have a form for.

Triggers:
1. On encounter start (T0 announcement): a full-screen dispatch notice establishes the assignment — unlicensed inherent casting, Loom Quarter tenement, bring in the subject.
2. On encounter start (T1 aigm): cues the AI GM to open with the street outside the tenement — the boarded windows, the locked door, the Concordat overseer visible in the alley mouth trying to look like he belongs there.
3. Patrol corridor perception check (T2, DC 12): when the player enters the outer corridor, a perception check — pass: they notice the patrolling soldier's shadow moving behind the interior door before he sees them, giving the player a moment to act first; fail: the soldier spots the player simultaneously and calls out, alerting the two inside.
4. Interior threshold combat trigger (T3): when the player crosses into the interior common room, the two soldiers inside flip hostile — they were willing to deal, but the moment a Cape walks in without negotiating, they decide they'd rather fight than share the bounty.
5. Alley speech (T4): when the player enters the alley, the Concordat overseer speaks — he names his authority, produces his own paperwork, and demands the player surrender the subject to Concordat custody.
6. On encounter completed (T5 xp): 150 XP awarded — the assignment is closed, whatever shape the closing took.
objective: Locate and detain the unlicensed elf in the tenement interior. Secure the scene before the Concordat overseer in the alley can claim jurisdiction. File the Regulate paperwork. The subject must be alive.

then.text:
BUREAU DISPATCH — LOOM QUARTER
Unlicensed inherent casting reported. Subject: one elf, female, apprentice weaver. Location: tenement, Loom Quarter yard. Orders: Reveal and Regulate. Bring the subject in breathing.

then.message: Open on the outside of the tenement. The windows are boarded from within. The front door is shut but not barred. At the far end of the rear alley a figure in dark grey — Concordat colours, no badge showing — leans against the wall pretending to read a folded paper. He has been there long enough that the neighbours are stepping around him. The player arrives from the north corridor.

onPass.message: You catch the shift of a shadow under the interior door — the patrolling soldier hasn't seen you yet. You have a breath to decide how to play this.

then.text: Cape. I have a Concordat writ, dated this morning, for the registered elf known as Lira of the Loom Quarter. She falls under Quota administration. You will surrender her to my custody — or I will note your badge number and file a jurisdictional complaint with the Bureau before sundown.
